briefing.headline: Rig up a Ned Rig — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Ned Rig is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: In stained water, use a chartreuse jig head on the traditional Ned rig for added visual edge with a 3-inch stick bait.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In stained water, use a chartreuse jig head on the traditional Ned rig for added visual edge with a 3-inch stick bait.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Texas rig a Ned bait on a swing head jig to fish snaggy areas where an exposed hook empties your terminal box.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: '2008 Classic at Hartwell — brutal cold Day 1, hammered them cranking a weighted-to-suspend Series 5 over 15-25ft with stop-and-start retrieve. Day 2, post-frontal sunny skies killed that bite. Ran to the upper end with stained water and fish...

Wheeler: Big fish spots are big giant flats in 22-29 ft with tiny isolated rockpiles — a boulder in the middle of a rocky flat, or a hard-bottom spot. Only good for 1-2 fish and time-consuming to find, but that's what separates you

Hackney: 'For winter deep-water jig fishing on lowland reservoirs with stumps and channel swings in 6-8 feet, I use 16-20 pound fluorocarbon. On hard structure like rock I go 14-16 pound. Only downsize to 12 in extremely deep clear water'

Johnston: In cold water when fishing shallower 2- to 10-foot depths, anchor outside of a sand patch, boulder, or weed edge and work the area hard for multiple hookups. These bass are moving super slow — you really have to work an area over hard wi...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
